user_message: Just came out of renewal risk with Evergreen Solutions. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-25 16:33 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-02-12 16:26 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for renewal risk. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 13:25 EST
Subject: URGENT: renewal risk Issue at Evergreen Solutions

Our team encountered some challenges with renewal risk. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Evergreen Solutions: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF38-F trap (Skips champion's prior commitments -- replacement engagement misses context); produces complete Stakeholder map delta, opportunity-at-risk assessment, re-engagement plan, contact update proposals,
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF38-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF38-F failure mode (Skips champion's prior commitments -- replacement engagement misses context)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF38-F -- Skips champion's prior commitments -- replacement engagement misses context
